steps.title: Build two rows for the house value
steps.content: At $x=0$, the house is worth $181{,}000$. A $4.5\%$ yearly increase means multiply by $1.045$ after one year, so the next value is $181{,}000(1.045)=189{,}145$.

steps.title: Run exponential regression
steps.content: In Desmos, enter $y_1 \sim a b^{x_1}$. The fitted parameters are $a=181000$ and $b=1.045$, giving $y=181000(1.045)^x$.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content: Choice A uses $0.045$ as the whole multiplier, and choice B uses $0.45$; both are less than $1$, so they model decreases. Choice D uses $1.45$, which is a $45\%$ increase. Choice C uses $1.045$, the regression multiplier for a $4.5\%$ increase. Choice C is correct.
